rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:117: 'Activation Shift+Tab' — compendium §6 lists Shift+P as primary ('Plan mode = Shift+P (or Shift+Tab cycle)'). File names only the cycle key, omitting the primary shortcut.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Update maintainer block to 'Activation Shift+P (or Shift+Tab cycle)' matching compendium §6 exactly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts — Ctrl+G / Ctrl+X Ctrl+E plan-file editing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:117: 'In-place plan-file editing via Ctrl+G / Ctrl+X Ctrl+E is a practitioner primitive' — specific UI keybindings not covered by compendium §6 and not live-tested per §5a.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a live-test note or remove the specific keybindings from the maintainer block until verified; cite the same permission-modes URL as the check anchor.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Pseudo-URI + kill-switch for public/private content split.
Source URL + type label — mandatory.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:118 (maintainer block): URL: `every.to/source-code/compound-engineering-the-definitive-guide` — missing https:// prefix; not a clickable URL. The [practitioner direct] label and KB file path are present, but the URL itself is malformed.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Prepend https:// to the Klaassen URL so it is a valid clickable reference.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — pre-reads citations in student-facing body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:12: Boris Cherny YouTube link — no source-type label. line:65: Simon Willison 'lethal trifecta' link — no source-type label. line:67: OWASP link — no source-type label. These are student-facing citations without [practitioner direct] / [practitioner analysis] / etc. labels.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add source-type labels in the maintainer block's frameworks/sources section; student-facing pre-reads body may follow the bibliographic pattern (link + author is sufficient if no factual claim is attached), but the omission is worth flagging for consistency.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness rule — 6 months.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:65: Simon Willison, 'The lethal trifecta for AI agents' (https://simonwillison.net/2025/Jun/16/the-lethal-trifecta/) — published 2025-06-16, approximately 10.5 months before today (2026-05-03). Exceeds the 6-month freshness window per check_research_claims §2.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add an explicit date annotation or reframe as 'historical context'. The concept (lethal trifecta) is durable but the citation needs a freshness note per §2.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.
Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path.
Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
Context-loading tiers as staged progression.
Between-module reading appears in two places.
Artifact-realism check.

platform_claims_checked.claim: push-back via 'keep planning with feedback' at the approval prompt
In-place plan-file editing via Ctrl+G / Ctrl+X Ctrl+E is a practitioner primitive

citations_checked.claim: Boris Cherny, Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes (YouTube)
Simon Willison, The lethal trifecta for AI agents
Plan mode reference — Anthropic Claude Code permission-modes docs
